intro: Nanodes schlechterianum, also known as Nanodes or Schlechter's Nanodes, is a plant species in the orchid family (Orchidaceae) endemic to New Guinea. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Nanodes schlechterianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Nanodes schlechterianum include infusions and decoctions, as well as tinctures and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
